text: COAR resource type: artistic work (COAR ID: F8RT-TJK0). Hierarchy path: artistic work. This is a top-level resource type. Definition: A work of visual arts and performing arts, including musical works; dramatic works; pantomimes and choreographic works; motion picture and other audiovisual works; pictorial, graphic, sculptural and architectural works. Adapted from Law Insider: https://www.lawinsider.com/dictionary/artistic-works

COAR resource type: cartographic material (COAR ID: c_12cc). Hierarchy path: cartographic material. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: map. Definition: Any material representing the whole or part of the earth or any celestial body at any scale. Cartographic materials include two- and three-dimensional maps and plans (including maps of imaginary places); aeronautical, navigational, and celestial charts; atlases; globes; block diagrams; sections; aerial photographs with a cartographic purpose; bird's-eye views (map views), etc. [Source: http://www.loc.gov/marc/cfmap.html]

COAR resource type: map (COAR ID: c_12cd). Hierarchy path: cartographic material > map. Parent type: cartographic material. Belongs to category: cartographic material. Definition: Defined as a representation normally to scale and on a flat medium, of a selection of material or abstract features on, or in relation to, the surface of the earth or of another celestial body. [Source: https://www.loc.gov/marc/bibliographic/bd007a.html]

COAR resource type: collection (COAR ID: RMP5-3GQ6). Hierarchy path: collection. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: archival collection, court documents. Definition: An aggregation of resources, which may encompass collections of one resourceType as well as those of mixed types. A collection is described as a group; its parts may also be separately described.

COAR resource type: archival collection (COAR ID: YC9F-HGCF). Hierarchy path: collection > archival collection. Parent type: collection. Belongs to category: collection. Definition: A collection of historical records and primary source documents. Documents may be tangible or digital.

COAR resource type: court documents (COAR ID: 1YTN-RJZE). Hierarchy path: collection > court documents. Parent type: collection. Belongs to category: collection. Definition: A set of judicial proceedings that record, physically or digitally, and in an orderly fashion, the development of a process or procedural actions so that they may be recorded and examined by the parties.

COAR resource type: dataset (COAR ID: c_ddb1). Hierarchy path: dataset. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: aggregated data, clinical trial data, compiled data, encoded data, experimental data, genomic data, geospatial data, laboratory notebook, measurement and test data, observational data, recorded data, simulation data, survey data. Definition: A collection of related facts and data encoded in a defined structure. [Source: Adapted from http://purl.org/spar/fabio/Dataset]

COAR resource type: aggregated data (COAR ID: ACF7-8YT9). Hierarchy path: dataset > aggregated data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Statistics that relate to broad classes, groups, or categories. The data are averaged, totaled, or otherwise derived from individual-level data.

COAR resource type: clinical trial data (COAR ID: c_cb28). Hierarchy path: dataset > clinical trial data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Data resulting from a research study in which one or more human subjects are prospectively assigned to one or more interventions to evaluate the effects of those interventions on health-related biomedical or behavioral outcomes.

COAR resource type: compiled data (COAR ID: FXF3-D3G7). Hierarchy path: dataset > compiled data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Data collected or assembled from multiple, often heterogeneous sources that have one or more reference points in common, and at least one of the sources was originally produced for other purposes.

COAR resource type: encoded data (COAR ID: AM6W-6QAW). Hierarchy path: dataset > encoded data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Qualitative data originally produced for other purposes converted into quantitative data by using coding techniques in accordance with pre-defined categorization schemes.

COAR resource type: experimental data (COAR ID: 63NG-B465). Hierarchy path: dataset > experimental data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Data resulting from the experimental research method involving the manipulation of some or all of the independent variables included in the hypotheses.

COAR resource type: genomic data (COAR ID: A8F1-NPV9). Hierarchy path: dataset > genomic data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Genomic data refers to the genome and DNA data of an organism. They are used in bioinformatics for collecting, storing and processing the genomes of living things.

COAR resource type: geospatial data (COAR ID: 2H0M-X761). Hierarchy path: dataset > geospatial data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Geospatial data are any type of data with spatial coordinates that allow them to be mapped to the Earth's surface. They can represent physical objects, discrete areas or continuous surfaces.

COAR resource type: laboratory notebook (COAR ID: H41Y-FW7B). Hierarchy path: dataset > laboratory notebook. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: A laboratory notebook is a primary record of research. Researchers use a lab notebook to document their hypotheses, experiments and initial analysis or interpretation of these experiments.

COAR resource type: measurement and test data (COAR ID: DD58-GFSX). Hierarchy path: dataset > measurement and test data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Data resulting from assessing specific properties of beings, things, phenomena, and/or processes by applying pre-established standards and/or specialized instruments or techniques.

COAR resource type: observational data (COAR ID: FF4C-28RK). Hierarchy path: dataset > observational data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Data resulting from observational research, which involves collecting observations as they occur, without attempting to manipulate any of the independent variables.

COAR resource type: recorded data (COAR ID: CQMR-7K63). Hierarchy path: dataset > recorded data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Data registered by mechanical or electronic means, in a form that allows the information to be retrieved and/or reproduced. For example, images or sounds on disc or magnetic tape.

COAR resource type: simulation data (COAR ID: W2XT-7017). Hierarchy path: dataset > simulation data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Data resulting from modeling or imitative representation of real-world processes, events, or systems, often using computer programs.

COAR resource type: survey data (COAR ID: NHD0-W6SY). Hierarchy path: dataset > survey data. Parent type: dataset. Belongs to category: dataset. Definition: Data resulting from a survey defined as an investigation about the characteristics of a given population by means of collecting data from a sample of that population.

COAR resource type: design (COAR ID: 542X-3S04). Hierarchy path: design. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: industrial design, layout design. Definition: Plans, drawing or set of drawings showing how something is to be made and how it will work and look.

COAR resource type: industrial design (COAR ID: JBNF-DYAD). Hierarchy path: design > industrial design. Parent type: design. Belongs to category: design. Definition: Industrial designs refer to the ornamental or aesthetic aspects of a useful article, including compositions of lines or colors or any three-dimensional forms that give a special appearance to a product or handicraft.

COAR resource type: layout design (COAR ID: BW7T-YM2G). Hierarchy path: design > layout design. Parent type: design. Belongs to category: design. Definition: Layout-design (topography) means the three-dimensional disposition of the elements of an integrated circuit and of some or all of the interconnections of an integrated circuit.

COAR resource type: image (COAR ID: c_c513). Hierarchy path: image. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: moving image, still image. Definition: A visual representation other than text, including all types of moving image and still image. [Source: Adapted from http://purl.org/dc/dcmitype/Image]

COAR resource type: moving image (COAR ID: c_8a7e). Hierarchy path: image > moving image. Parent type: image. Belongs to category: image. Subtypes include: video. Definition: A moving display, either generated dynamically by a computer program or formed from a series of pre-recorded still images imparting an impression of motion when shown in succession.

COAR resource type: video (COAR ID: c_12ce). Hierarchy path: image > moving image > video. Parent type: moving image. Belongs to category: image > moving image. Definition: Media that makes use of images (usually moving), with or without sound, over a period of playback time.

COAR resource type: still image (COAR ID: c_ecc8). Hierarchy path: image > still image. Parent type: image. Belongs to category: image. Definition: A recorded static visual representation. This class of image includes diagrams, drawings, graphs, graphic designs, plans, photographs and prints.

COAR resource type: interactive resource (COAR ID: c_e9a0). Hierarchy path: interactive resource. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: website. Definition: A resource requiring interaction from the user to be understood, executed, or experienced. Examples include forms on Web pages, applets, multimedia learning objects, chat services, or virtual reality environments.

COAR resource type: website (COAR ID: c_7ad9). Hierarchy path: interactive resource > website. Parent type: interactive resource. Belongs to category: interactive resource. Definition: A collection of related web pages containing text, images, videos and/or other digital assets that are addressed relative to a common Uniform Resource Locator (URL).

COAR resource type: knowledge organization system (COAR ID: GSZA-Y7V7). Hierarchy path: knowledge organization system. This is a top-level resource type. Definition: All types of schemes for organizing information and promoting knowledge management, including classification schemes, subject headings, authority files, semantic artifacts, semantic networks and ontologies.

COAR resource type: learning object (COAR ID: c_e059). Hierarchy path: learning object. This is a top-level resource type. Definition: A digital resource that can be reused to enhance teaching and learning.
COAR resource type: other (COAR ID: c_1843). Hierarchy path: other. This is a top-level resource type. Definition: A resource type that is not included in existing terms. [COAR definition]

COAR resource type: patent (COAR ID: c_15cd). Hierarchy path: patent. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: PCT application, design patent, plant patent, plant variety protection, software patent, utility model. Definition: A set of exclusive rights granted by law to applicants for inventions that are new, non-obvious and commercially applicable. A patent is valid for a limited period (generally 20 years).

COAR resource type: PCT application (COAR ID: SB3Y-W4EH). Hierarchy path: patent > PCT application. Parent type: patent. Belongs to category: patent. Definition: A patent application filed through the WIPO-administered Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT), also known as an international application.

COAR resource type: design patent (COAR ID: C53B-JCY5). Hierarchy path: patent > design patent. Parent type: patent. Belongs to category: patent. Definition: A patent granted to any person who has invented any new and non-obvious ornamental design for an article of manufacture. The design patent protects only the appearance of an article, but not its structural or functional features.

COAR resource type: plant patent (COAR ID: Z907-YMBB). Hierarchy path: patent > plant patent. Parent type: patent. Belongs to category: patent. Definition: A patent granted to anyone who has invented or discovered and asexually reproduced any distinct and new variety of plant, including cultivated sports, mutants, hybrids, and newly found seedlings.

COAR resource type: plant variety protection (COAR ID: GPQ7-G5VE). Hierarchy path: patent > plant variety protection. Parent type: patent. Belongs to category: patent. Definition: Plant variety protection, also called a plant breeder's right (PBR), is a form of intellectual property right granted to the breeder of a new plant variety.

COAR resource type: software patent (COAR ID: MW8G-3CR8). Hierarchy path: patent > software patent. Parent type: patent. Belongs to category: patent. Definition: A patent for a software invention that fulfills substantive patentability criteria (novelty, inventive step and industrial applicability).

COAR resource type: utility model (COAR ID: 9DKX-KSAF). Hierarchy path: patent > utility model. Parent type: patent. Belongs to category: patent. Definition: A special form of patent right with a shorter term of protection and less stringent patentability requirements than normal patents. Also known as petty patents or short-term patents.

COAR resource type: physical sample (COAR ID: S7R1-K5P0). Hierarchy path: physical sample. This is a top-level resource type. Definition: Physical samples include biological specimens, rock or mineral specimens, soil or sediment cores, plants and seeds, water quality samples, archaeological artefacts, or DNA and human tissue samples.

COAR resource type: research instrument (COAR ID: 8KJG-QS0Y). Hierarchy path: research instrument. This is a top-level resource type. Definition: A device, tool or apparatus used to obtain, measure and/or analyze data.

COAR resource type: software (COAR ID: c_5ce6). Hierarchy path: software. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: research software, source code. Definition: A computer program in source code (text) or compiled form. [Source: http://purl.org/dc/dcmitype/Software]

COAR resource type: research software (COAR ID: c_c950). Hierarchy path: software > research software. Parent type: software. Belongs to category: software. Definition: Software that is used to generate, process or analyse results that you intend to appear in a publication. Research software can be anything from a few lines of code to a professionally developed software package.

COAR resource type: source code (COAR ID: QH80-2R4E). Hierarchy path: software > source code. Parent type: software. Belongs to category: software. Definition: Source code is any collection of code, with or without comments, written using a human-readable programming language, usually as plain text.

COAR resource type: sound (COAR ID: c_18cc). Hierarchy path: sound. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: musical composition. Definition: A resource primarily intended to be heard. Examples include a music playback file format, an audio compact disc, and recorded speech or sounds.

COAR resource type: musical composition (COAR ID: c_18cd). Hierarchy path: sound > musical composition. Parent type: sound. Belongs to category: sound. Definition: Musical composition can refer to an original piece of music, the structure of a musical piece, or the process of creating a new piece of music.

COAR resource type: text (COAR ID: c_18cf). Hierarchy path: text. This is a top-level resource type. Subtypes include: annotation, bibliography, blog post, book, conference output, journal, lecture, letter, magazine, manuscript, musical notation, newspaper, other periodical, preprint, report, research proposal, review, technical documentation, thesis, transcription, working paper. Definition: A resource consisting primarily of words for reading. Examples include books, letters, dissertations, poems, newspapers, articles, archives of mailing lists.

COAR resource type: annotation (COAR ID: c_1162). Hierarchy path: text > annotation. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: An annotation in the sense of a legal note is a legally explanatory comment on a decision handed down by a court or arbitral tribunal.

COAR resource type: bibliography (COAR ID: c_86bc). Hierarchy path: text > bibliography. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: A list of the books and articles that have been used by someone when writing a particular book or article.

COAR resource type: blog post (COAR ID: c_6947). Hierarchy path: text > blog post. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: A piece of writing or other item of content published on a blog.

COAR resource type: book (COAR ID: c_2f33). Hierarchy path: text > book. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Subtypes include: book part. Definition: A non-serial publication that is complete in one volume or a designated finite number of volumes.

COAR resource type: book part (COAR ID: c_3248). Hierarchy path: text > book > book part. Parent type: book. Belongs to category: text > book. Definition: A defined chapter or section of a book, usually with a separate title or number.

COAR resource type: conference output (COAR ID: c_c94f). Hierarchy path: text > conference output. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Subtypes include: conference paper not in proceedings, conference poster not in proceedings, conference presentation, conference proceedings. Definition: All kind of digital resources contributed to a conference, like conference presentation (slides), conference report, conference lecture, abstracts, demonstrations.

COAR resource type: conference paper not in proceedings (COAR ID: c_18cp). Hierarchy path: text > conference output > conference paper not in proceedings. Parent type: conference output. Belongs to category: text > conference output. Definition: A paper, typically the realization of a research paper reporting original research findings. Use this label when the paper is not published in a proceeding.

metadata.label: conference paper not in proceedings
metadata.breadcrumb: text > conference output > conference paper not in proceedings

text: COAR resource type: conference poster not in proceedings (COAR ID: c_18co). Hierarchy path: text > conference output > conference poster not in proceedings. Parent type: conference output. Belongs to category: text > conference output. Definition: A display poster, typically containing text with illustrative figures and/or tables, usually reporting research results or proposing hypotheses. Use this label when the poster is not published in a proceeding.

metadata.label: conference poster not in proceedings
metadata.breadcrumb: text > conference output > conference poster not in proceedings

text: COAR resource type: conference presentation (COAR ID: R60J-J5BD). Hierarchy path: text > conference output > conference presentation. Parent type: conference output. Belongs to category: text > conference output. Definition: A set of slides containing text, tables or figures, designed to communicate ideas or research results, for projection and viewing by an audience at a conference, symposium, seminar, lecture, workshop or other gatherings.

COAR resource type: conference proceedings (COAR ID: c_f744). Hierarchy path: text > conference output > conference proceedings. Parent type: conference output. Belongs to category: text > conference output. Subtypes include: conference paper, conference poster. Definition: Conference proceedings is the official record of a conference meeting. It is a collection of documents which corresponds to the presentations given at the conference.

COAR resource type: conference paper (COAR ID: c_5794). Hierarchy path: text > conference output > conference proceedings > conference paper. Parent type: conference proceedings. Belongs to category: text > conference output > conference proceedings. Definition: A paper, published within a conference proceeding, typically the realization of a research paper reporting original research findings.

metadata.label: conference paper
metadata.breadcrumb: text > conference output > conference proceedings > conference paper

text: COAR resource type: conference poster (COAR ID: c_6670). Hierarchy path: text > conference output > conference proceedings > conference poster. Parent type: conference proceedings. Belongs to category: text > conference output > conference proceedings. Definition: A display poster, published within a conference proceeding, typically containing text with illustrative figures and/or tables, usually reporting research results or proposing hypotheses.

metadata.label: conference poster
metadata.breadcrumb: text > conference output > conference proceedings > conference poster

text: COAR resource type: journal (COAR ID: c_0640). Hierarchy path: text > journal. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Subtypes include: editorial, journal article, letter to the editor. Definition: A journal is a serial publication devoted to disseminating original research and current developments on a subject.

COAR resource type: editorial (COAR ID: c_b239). Hierarchy path: text > journal > editorial. Parent type: journal. Belongs to category: text > journal. Definition: A brief essay expressing the opinion or position of the chief editor(s) of a journal with respect to a current political, social, cultural, or professional issue.

COAR resource type: journal article (COAR ID: c_6501). Hierarchy path: text > journal > journal article. Parent type: journal. Belongs to category: text > journal. Subtypes include: corrigendum, data paper, research article, review article, software paper. Definition: An article, typically the realization of a research paper reporting original research findings, published in a journal issue.

COAR resource type: corrigendum (COAR ID: c_7acd). Hierarchy path: text > journal > journal article > corrigendum. Parent type: journal article. Belongs to category: text > journal > journal article. Definition: A formal correction to an error introduced by the author into a previously published document.

COAR resource type: data paper (COAR ID: c_beb9). Hierarchy path: text > journal > journal article > data paper. Parent type: journal article. Belongs to category: text > journal > journal article. Definition: A data paper is a scholarly publication describing a particular dataset or group of datasets, published in the form of a peer-reviewed article in a scholarly journal. The main purpose of a data paper is to describe data, the circumstances of their collection, and information related to data features, access and potential reuse.

metadata.label: data paper
metadata.breadcrumb: text > journal > journal article > data paper

text: COAR resource type: research article (COAR ID: c_2df8fbb1). Hierarchy path: text > journal > journal article > research article. Parent type: journal article. Belongs to category: text > journal > journal article. Definition: A research article is a primary source that reports the methods and results of an original study performed by the authors.

metadata.label: research article
metadata.breadcrumb: text > journal > journal article > research article

text: COAR resource type: review article (COAR ID: c_dcae04bc). Hierarchy path: text > journal > journal article > review article. Parent type: journal article. Belongs to category: text > journal > journal article. Definition: A review article is a secondary source that is written about other articles, and does not report original research of its own.

metadata.label: review article
metadata.breadcrumb: text > journal > journal article > review article

text: COAR resource type: software paper (COAR ID: c_7bab). Hierarchy path: text > journal > journal article > software paper. Parent type: journal article. Belongs to category: text > journal > journal article. Definition: A software paper should include the rationale for the development of the tool and details of the code used for its construction.

metadata.label: software paper
metadata.breadcrumb: text > journal > journal article > software paper

text: COAR resource type: letter to the editor (COAR ID: c_545b). Hierarchy path: text > journal > letter to the editor. Parent type: journal. Belongs to category: text > journal. Definition: A letter addressed to the editor and comments on or discusses an item previously published by that periodical, or of interest to its readership.

metadata.label: letter to the editor
metadata.breadcrumb: text > journal > letter to the editor

text: COAR resource type: lecture (COAR ID: c_8544). Hierarchy path: text > lecture. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: Transcription of an oral presentation intended to present information or teach people about a particular subject, for example by a university or college teacher.

COAR resource type: letter (COAR ID: c_0857). Hierarchy path: text > letter. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: A brief description of important new research, also known as communication.

COAR resource type: magazine (COAR ID: c_2cd9). Hierarchy path: text > magazine. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Subtypes include: magazine article. Definition: A popular interest periodical usually containing articles on a variety of topics, written by various authors in a nonscholarly style or a trade publication covering a specific topic for people who work in that field or industry.

COAR resource type: magazine article (COAR ID: 43KC-T6DC). Hierarchy path: text > magazine > magazine article. Parent type: magazine. Belongs to category: text > magazine. Definition: An item appearing in a magazine (a general interest or specific focus periodical). This item is often shorter in length, submitted by in-house and external authors.

COAR resource type: manuscript (COAR ID: c_0040). Hierarchy path: text > manuscript. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: A manuscript is a work of any kind (text, inscription, music score, map, etc.) written entirely by hand.

COAR resource type: musical notation (COAR ID: c_18cw). Hierarchy path: text > musical notation. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: Symbols used to write music, as in a music score, and to express mathematical concepts.

COAR resource type: newspaper (COAR ID: c_2fe3). Hierarchy path: text > newspaper. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Subtypes include: newspaper article. Definition: A non-peer reviewed periodical, usually published daily or weekly, consisting primarily of editorials and news items concerning current or recent events and matters of public interest.

COAR resource type: newspaper article (COAR ID: c_998f). Hierarchy path: text > newspaper > newspaper article. Parent type: newspaper. Belongs to category: text > newspaper. Definition: Work consisting of a news item appearing in a general-interest newspaper or other general news periodical, containing information of current and timely interest in a field.

COAR resource type: other periodical (COAR ID: QX5C-AR31). Hierarchy path: text > other periodical. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: A resource type that is not included in existing terms under the top concept Text. [COAR definition]

COAR resource type: preprint (COAR ID: c_816b). Hierarchy path: text > preprint. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: A preprint is a scientific manuscript without peer-review and has not yet been accepted by a journal, typically submitted to a public server/repository by the author.

COAR resource type: report (COAR ID: c_93fc). Hierarchy path: text > report. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Subtypes include: clinical study, data management plan, knowledge synthesis protocol, memorandum, policy report, project deliverable, research protocol, research report, technical report. Definition: A report is a separately published record of research findings, research still in progress, policy developments and events, or other technical findings, usually bearing a report number.

COAR resource type: clinical study (COAR ID: c_7877). Hierarchy path: text > report > clinical study. Parent type: report. Belongs to category: text > report. Definition: A work that reports on the results of a research study to evaluate interventions or exposures on biomedical or health-related outcomes. The two main types are interventional studies (clinical trials) and observational studies.

COAR resource type: data management plan (COAR ID: c_ab20). Hierarchy path: text > report > data management plan. Parent type: report. Belongs to category: text > report. Definition: A formal statement describing how research data will be managed and documented throughout a research project and the terms regarding the subsequent deposit of the data with a data repository for long-term management and preservation.

COAR resource type: knowledge synthesis protocol (COAR ID: DX5J-TA9R). Hierarchy path: text > report > knowledge synthesis protocol. Parent type: report. Belongs to category: text > report. Definition: In the context of systematic reviews and meta-analyses, a protocol is a document that presents an explicit plan for a systematic review, detailing the rationale and a priori methodological and analytical approach.

COAR resource type: memorandum (COAR ID: c_18wz). Hierarchy path: text > report > memorandum. Parent type: report. Belongs to category: text > report. Definition: A formal note distributed internally to one or more persons in a company, agency, organization, or institution, with a header indicating the date, addressee, sender, and subject of the text.

COAR resource type: policy report (COAR ID: c_186u). Hierarchy path: text > report > policy report. Parent type: report. Belongs to category: text > report. Definition: A policy report presents what is known about a particular issue or problem. It assembles facts and evidence to help readers understand complex issues and form a response.

COAR resource type: project deliverable (COAR ID: c_18op). Hierarchy path: text > report > project deliverable. Parent type: report. Belongs to category: text > report. Definition: A document containing a project report, intended to be delivered to a customer or funding agency describing the results achieved within a specific project.

COAR resource type: research protocol (COAR ID: YZ1N-ZFT9). Hierarchy path: text > report > research protocol. Parent type: report. Belongs to category: text > report. Definition: The protocol is a detailed plan of the research study including a project summary, project description covering the rationale, objectives, methodology, data management and analysis, ethical considerations, gender issues and references.

COAR resource type: research report (COAR ID: c_18ws). Hierarchy path: text > report > research report. Parent type: report. Belongs to category: text > report. Definition: A publication that reports on the findings of a research project or scientific observations on or about a subject.

COAR resource type: technical report (COAR ID: c_18gh). Hierarchy path: text > report > technical report. Parent type: report. Belongs to category: text > report. Definition: A document that describes the process, progress, or results of technical or scientific research or the state of a technical or scientific research problem. It might also include recommendations and conclusions of the research.

COAR resource type: research proposal (COAR ID: c_baaf). Hierarchy path: text > research proposal. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: A research proposal is a document proposing a research project, generally in the sciences or academia, and generally constitutes a request for sponsorship of that research.

COAR resource type: review (COAR ID: c_efa0). Hierarchy path: text > review. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Subtypes include: book review, commentary, peer review. Definition: A review of others' published work.

COAR resource type: book review (COAR ID: c_ba08). Hierarchy path: text > review > book review. Parent type: review. Belongs to category: text > review. Definition: A written review and critical analysis of the content, scope and quality of a book or other monographic work.

COAR resource type: commentary (COAR ID: D97F-VB57). Hierarchy path: text > review > commentary. Parent type: review. Belongs to category: text > review. Definition: A commentary is a more in-depth analysis written to draw attention to a work already published. Commentaries are somewhat like reviews in that the author presents his or her analysis of a work and why it would be of interest to a specific audience.

COAR resource type: peer review (COAR ID: H9BQ-739P). Hierarchy path: text > review > peer review. Parent type: review. Belongs to category: text > review. Definition: An evaluation of scientific, academic, or professional work by others working in the same field.

COAR resource type: technical documentation (COAR ID: c_71bd). Hierarchy path: text > technical documentation. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: Technical documentation refers to any type of documentation that describes handling, functionality and architecture of a technical product or a product under development or use.

COAR resource type: thesis (COAR ID: c_46ec). Hierarchy path: text > thesis. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Subtypes include: bachelor thesis, doctoral thesis, master thesis. Definition: A book authored by a student containing a formal presentations of research outputs submitted for examination in completion of a course of study at an institution of higher education, to fulfil the requirements for an academic degree. Also known as a dissertation.

COAR resource type: bachelor thesis (COAR ID: c_7a1f). Hierarchy path: text > thesis > bachelor thesis. Parent type: thesis. Belongs to category: text > thesis. Definition: A thesis reporting a research project undertaken as part of an undergraduate course of education leading to a bachelor's degree.

COAR resource type: doctoral thesis (COAR ID: c_db06). Hierarchy path: text > thesis > doctoral thesis. Parent type: thesis. Belongs to category: text > thesis. Definition: A thesis reporting the research undertaken during a period of graduate study leading to a doctoral degree.

COAR resource type: master thesis (COAR ID: c_bdcc). Hierarchy path: text > thesis > master thesis. Parent type: thesis. Belongs to category: text > thesis. Definition: A thesis reporting a research project undertaken as part of a graduate course of education leading to a master's degree.

COAR resource type: transcription (COAR ID: 6NC7-GK9S). Hierarchy path: text > transcription. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: A written record of words spoken in court proceedings or in a speech, interview, broadcast, or sound recording.

COAR resource type: working paper (COAR ID: c_8042). Hierarchy path: text > working paper. Parent type: text. Belongs to category: text. Definition: A working or discussion paper circulated publicly or among a group of peers. Certain disciplines, for example economics, issue working papers in series.

COAR resource type: trademark (COAR ID: H6QP-SC1X). Hierarchy path: trademark. This is a top-level resource type. Definition: A sign used to distinguish the goods or services of one undertaking from those of others. A trademark may consist of words and combinations of words, logos, figures and images, letters, numbers, sounds, or smells.

COAR resource type: workflow (COAR ID: c_393c). Hierarchy path: workflow. This is a top-level resource type. Definition: A recorded sequence of connected steps, which may be automated, specifying a reliably repeatable sequence of operations to be undertaken when conducting a particular job, for example an in silico investigation that extracts and processes information from a number of bioinformatics databases.

COAR resource type 'cartographic material' and all subtypes: map (c_12cd). Definition: Any material representing the whole or part of the earth or any celestial body at any scale. Cartographic materials include two- and three-dimensional maps and plans (including maps of imaginary places); aeronautical, navigational, and celestial charts; atlases; globes; block diagrams; sections; aerial photographs with a cartographic purpose; bird's-eye views (map views), etc. [Source: http://www.loc.gov/marc/cfmap.html]

COAR resource type 'collection' and all subtypes: archival collection (YC9F-HGCF), court documents (1YTN-RJZE). Definition: An aggregation of resources, which may encompass collections of one resourceType as well as those of mixed types. A collection is described as a group; its parts may also be separately described.

COAR resource type 'dataset' and all subtypes: aggregated data (ACF7-8YT9), clinical trial data (c_cb28), compiled data (FXF3-D3G7), encoded data (AM6W-6QAW), experimental data (63NG-B465), genomic data (A8F1-NPV9), geospatial data (2H0M-X761), laboratory notebook (H41Y-FW7B), measurement and test data (DD58-GFSX), observational data (FF4C-28RK), recorded data (CQMR-7K63), simulation data (W2XT-7017), survey data (NHD0-W6SY). Definition: A collection of related facts and data encoded in a defined structure. [Source: Adapted from http://purl.org/spar/fabio/Dataset]

COAR resource type 'design' and all subtypes: industrial design (JBNF-DYAD), layout design (BW7T-YM2G). Definition: Plans, drawing or set of drawings showing how something is to be made and how it will work and look.

COAR resource type 'image' and all subtypes: moving image (c_8a7e), video (c_12ce), still image (c_ecc8). Definition: A visual representation other than text, including all types of moving image and still image. [Source: Adapted from http://purl.org/dc/dcmitype/Image]

COAR resource type 'interactive resource' and all subtypes: website (c_7ad9). Definition: A resource requiring interaction from the user to be understood, executed, or experienced. Examples include forms on Web pages, applets, multimedia learning objects, chat services, or virtual reality environments.

COAR resource type 'patent' and all subtypes: PCT application (SB3Y-W4EH), design patent (C53B-JCY5), plant patent (Z907-YMBB), plant variety protection (GPQ7-G5VE), software patent (MW8G-3CR8), utility model (9DKX-KSAF). Definition: A set of exclusive rights granted by law to applicants for inventions that are new, non-obvious and commercially applicable. A patent is valid for a limited period (generally 20 years).

COAR resource type 'software' and all subtypes: research software (c_c950), source code (QH80-2R4E). Definition: A computer program in source code (text) or compiled form. [Source: http://purl.org/dc/dcmitype/Software]

COAR resource type 'sound' and all subtypes: musical composition (c_18cd). Definition: A resource primarily intended to be heard. Examples include a music playback file format, an audio compact disc, and recorded speech or sounds.

Definition: A resource consisting primarily of words for reading. Examples include books, letters, dissertations, poems, newspapers, articles, archives of mailing lists.
